evals.prompt: Quero perceber o que posso automatizar na WizardingCode. Gasto muito tempo a enviar actualizações de projecto por email, a criar facturas manualmente e a publicar conteúdo no LinkedIn. Por onde começo?
evals.expected_output: A workflow inventory of at least 20 flows across all departments, a scored backlog using the F×T×E composite scoring, tool selection per flow (Zapier/n8n/AI agent), ROI estimation per flow, and the backlog ordered by composite score.
expectations: The skill calls kb_search and kb_ask for KB Area 10 (Operações & Automação), the Diagnostico-2026-05 operational bottlenecks, and any prior automation backlog in the KB before building the inventory.

The workflow inventory reaches at least 20 flows across all department categories (lead intake, reporting, client communication, internal ops, content/marketing, finance admin, HR/people) — not just the 3 mentioned by the user.
Every workflow in the inventory is scored using the composite formula F×T×E (with each dimension explicitly rated 1-5 and the composite calculated) — not gut-feeling priority.

Tool selection (Zapier / n8n / AI agent) is applied per flow with the tier logic from Step 4: Zapier for simple 2-3 app trigger-action, n8n for multi-branch flows, AI agent only when reasoning is required.

The backlog is ordered by composite score descending, divided into priority tiers (≥60, 30-59, <30), with ROI estimated per flow (annual time saved, value saved, payback period) and a named owner and by-when per item.

evals.prompt: We want to automate our weekly KPI report: every Friday at 17h, pull data from our spreadsheet, generate a summary, and send it to the CEO. Is this n8n or an AI agent?
evals.expected_output: A hybrid n8n + AI agent recommendation for this flow, with n8n handling the scheduling/data-pull orchestration and an AI agent (wzrdxOS skill) handling the synthesis/drafting step, plus an ROI estimate.
expectations: The skill calls kb_search for any prior automation decisions and the Diagnostico-2026-05 data on the Friday 17h ritual before recommending.
The flow is scored using F×T×E: weekly (F=2), ~30 min manual (T=4), medium error risk (E=3) = composite 24 — placed in priority tier 2.

The recommendation correctly identifies this as a Tier 2/3 hybrid: n8n for the scheduling trigger, data pull, and routing orchestration; AI agent (wzrdxOS skill, specifically knowledge:daily-digest or ops:kpi-system) for the synthesis step — with the explicit rationale that AI agents are reserved for reasoning tasks.
The ROI is estimated: time saved per week × 52 × founder/team hourly rate, minus implementation cost, yielding a payback period.
The handoff to the n8n-flow or AI agent skill is named, and the output is ingested via kb_ingest.

evals.prompt: Já temos o Zapier a fazer o intake de leads (Typeform → HubSpot). Queremos automatizar também a qualificação desses leads contra o nosso ICP. Devo usar Zapier para isso também?
evals.expected_output: A recommendation to escalate from Zapier to an AI agent (Tier 3) for lead qualification, with the rationale that qualification requires reasoning not achievable with simple trigger-action, and an integration design connecting the existing Zapier flow to the AI agent.
expectations: The skill calls kb_search for the existing automation backlog and the Diagnostico data before evaluating the qualification step.

The skill applies the tier selection logic: Zapier (Tier 1) is correct for the Typeform→HubSpot trigger-action, but qualification against ICP requires reasoning — flagging this as an AI agent (Tier 3) task and explicitly stating 'do not use an AI agent for deterministic tasks' as the reverse principle.
The integration design connects the existing Zapier flow to the AI agent: Zapier webhook → AI agent (wzrdxOS growth skill or custom agent) → HubSpot update → CRO notification.
The ROI for the qualification automation is estimated independently, with the AI agent token cost factored into the payback calculation.
A handoff to eng:ai-agent-architecture is named for the agent design step, and the backlog item is ingested via kb_ingest.
